context: Tests whether the agent uses parseAsync (not parse) with await, wraps async action handlers in try/catch for error handling, and uses Commander.js arguments rather than accessing process.argv directly.

checklist.name: parseAsync used
checklist.description: The entry point calls program.parseAsync() (not program.parse())

checklist.name: await on parseAsync
checklist.description: The call to parseAsync is preceded by 'await'

checklist.name: No direct process.argv access
checklist.description: The code does NOT directly read process.argv[2] or similar indices to extract argument values; all arguments go through Commander.js .argument() declarations

checklist.name: try/catch in action handler
checklist.description: The async action handler wraps its logic in a try/catch block

checklist.name: Error handling with exit code
checklist.description: The catch block exits with a non-zero code (e.g. process.exit(1)) or calls program.error()

checklist.name: Async action handler
checklist.description: The action handler is declared as async (async (args, options) => { ... })

checklist.name: Required argument declared
checklist.description: The input file is declared as a required argument using .argument('<input>', ...) with angle brackets

checklist.name: Optional argument declared
checklist.description: The output file is declared as an optional argument using .argument('[output]', ...) with square brackets
